q2_training_boundary: No RL, DPO, preference fine-tuning, or generator-level policy optimization is part of Q2.

feature_delta_contract.definition: winner_metric - loser_metric using candidate-level backend rows only
feature_delta_contract.missing_semantics: missing metrics are null with backend coverage status; missing is not zero

evidence_sources.name: backend_supported
evidence_sources.allowed_when: candidate-level RDKit and at least one score_only docking backend metric are present for both candidates

evidence_sources.name: proxy_only
evidence_sources.allowed_when: only heuristic pocket/contact/geometry proxy fields are present

evidence_sources.name: mixed_backend_proxy
evidence_sources.allowed_when: RDKit or docking exists for one side but not all required pair metrics

pair_examples.winner_condition: lower Vina or GNINA affinity, reasonable QED/SA, low clash_fraction
pair_examples.loser_condition: worse docking or clash while same example/protein

pair_examples.winner_condition: better docking despite lower or comparable pocket_contact_fraction

pair_examples.loser_condition: good docking but SA/QED or Lipinski violation unacceptable

pair_examples.winner_condition: raw or no_repair candidate with better score_only docking
pair_examples.loser_condition: repaired/reranked/full_repair candidate with improved proxy but degraded Vina/GNINA

layer_policy.reporting_rule: Preference pairs may compare layers, but layer names and candidate ids must be preserved; repaired/reranked wins must not be described as raw model-native wins.
